address.addrLine: The John Rylands Research Institute and Library

addrLine.ref: The John Rylands Research Institute and Library

msIdentifier.repository: The John Rylands Research Institute and Library

msDesc.head: Fourteen folios of paintings and calligraphy from an album of assembled at Lucknow for Swiss mercenary Antoine Polier (d. 1795).

msContents.summary: Fourteen folios of South Asian paintings and Persian calligraphy detached from an album assembled for Swiss mercenary Colonel Antoine-Louis-Henri Polier (d. 1795), probably at Lucknow and overseen by artist Mihr Chand, between 1780 to 1787. Disordered from its original sequence, the opening folio (now found on 9b) states the original album contained forty leaves. Despite its incomplete and rebound condition, it still constitutes a significant portion of one of Polier's albums to survive today.

msItem.title: Album of South Asian Painting and Persian Calligraphy of Antoine Polier (d. 1795)
Nobleman with moustache on a veranda holds a rose in his right hand.
Princess worships at a Yoni Lingham
Shikastah prose composition on dark brick red paper
Shikastah prose composition on marbled paper
Calligraphy by ‘Abd al-Rashid
msItem.note: Portrait format, written at an oblique angle. Handle with caution, breaking in the gutter margin

msItem.title: Nasta‘līq murrabkibāt
msItem.note: Chinese silk brocade hinge in the gutter may be a repair.

msItem.title: European scene signed by Mihr Chand
Princess smokes a hookah on a veranda
Shikastah murakkibāt (letter combinations in broken script)

supportDesc.support: All items uniformly mounted on thin laminates of at least six layers of ivory-coloured paper, probably handmade in Awadh, all sized and polished, then fully decorated (see decoration). All mounts interleaved with very thin, ivory-coloured, machine-made and calendered paper paper, probably manufactured in Britain, tipped onto the gutters.
supportDesc.foliation: Modern pencilled Arabic numbers, from left to right, on the upper-right corners of the a sides throughout.
supportDesc.collation: Collation disrupted and reconfigured when rebound, with Polier's ex libris, originally the opening folios, displaced on 6b. Currently comprised of primarily binions made by hinging the mounts together with thin cotton shirting adhered to the gutters.
supportDesc.condition: Underlying mounts in fair condition, with some damage to the edges and bumped ocrners. Interleaving in fair but stable condition, with occasional staining, soiling, and creasing. Moderate staining and insect damage to the central compositions.

layoutDesc.layout: All items individually mounted in either portrait or landscape format.

decoDesc.decoNote: Illumination: Folio 1b bears a scalloped domed headpiece with gilt palmette foliate scrollwork on an ultramarine ground and an uninscribed central cartouche, and four vertical radiating lines.

Floral Margins: Polychrome hand-painted floral scrollwork margins, all executed by the same, unidentified commercial artist, whose ubiquitous hand similarly embellish a majority of Polier's album folios.

Applied Margins: Strips of indigo, salmon pink, and slate-coloured papers, measuring ~9–10 mm wide, adhered as decorative borders then painted in gold and silver with various deigns frame all central compositions and folio perimeters.
Ruling: Folios 1b and 2a ruled in gold outlined with thin black lines, and surrounded by another single line. The margins of folios 2b onwards ruled with single lines of ultramarine blue.

physDesc.additions: Bookplates: Left pastedown: Bibliotheca Lindesiana with pencilled shelfmark F/3, and Bland MSS No. 20, with the name and number crossed out and Persian and 10 written aside.

binding.p: Probably rebound for former owner Alexander Lindsay, 25th Earl of Crawford (1812–1880), as the volume matches another album that he formerly owned (Indian Drawings 13), another ex-Polier album that Bland previously obtained from an unidentified source.

Sewn on five recessed cords. Case-bound, hollow-backed, covered in full, black levant-grained goatskin leather over pasteboards with light blue silk lining the board interiors. Earlier flyleaves of medium-weight, cross-grained, ivory-coloured, externally sized and polished paper probably handmade in Awadh, with heavily flocked, machine-made blue paper flyleaves added to the volume when restored.
binding.condition: Handle binding with caution. In fair but stable condition, with breakage in the gutter margins.

history.origin: Probably assembled by Mihr Chand for Colonel Antoine-Louis-Henri Polier (1741–1795) in Lucknow undated, but circa 1773–1780 CE.

provenance.p: Likely taken with Polier when he returned to Europe.
After Bland's death, London bookseller Bernard Quaritch (1819–1899) sold his oriental manuscripts to Alexander Lindsay, 25th Earl of Crawford (1812–1880) in 1866.
Purchased by Enriqueta Rylands (1843–1908) in 1901 from James Ludovic Lindsay, 26th Earl of Crawford (1847–1913).

acquisition.p: Bequeathed by Enriqueta Rylands (1843–1908) in 1908 to the John Rylands Library.

source.p: Bibliographical description based on a manuscript catalogue by Michael Kerney, circa 1890s.
Manuscript description by Jake Benson in 2026 with reference to the volume in hand, and in consultation with Friederike Weis, regarding Polier's other album in relation to this one.

availability.p: To book an in-person or online appointment to consult the manuscript, visit Using the Special Collections Reading Rooms. For any other enquiries please email uml.special-collections@manchester.ac.uk.

listBibl.bibl: F. Weis, ed. Eighteenth-Century Indian Muraqqaʿs: Audiences, Artists, Patrons and Collectors. Leiden: Brill, 2025.

revisionDesc.change: Jake Benson created preliminary record derived from Indian Drawings 12 and Persian MS 10.
